338	def classify_document_type(
339	    *,
340	    openalex_publication_type: str = "",
341	    openalex_source_type: str = "",
342	    openalex_is_peer_reviewed: "bool | None" = None,
343	    predatory_oa: bool = False,
344	    source_class: str = "",
345	    url: str = "",
346	    title: str = "",
347	    doi: str = "",
348	) -> "tuple[DocumentType, str]":
349	    """Classify a source's document GENRE deterministically (no LLM, no network).
350	
351	    Returns ``(DocumentType, basis)`` where ``basis`` is a short provenance string for audit.
352	
353	    Priority:
354	      1. OpenAlex GOLD genre signals (require ``source_type == "journal"`` AND peer-reviewed
355	         for the journal-POSITIVE verdict — ``publication_type`` alone over-marks ~99% as
356	         "article", so type alone is NOT trusted). ``predatory_oa`` short-circuits a
357	         predatory venue BEFORE the journal verdict.
358	      2. ``source_class`` secondary (field-agnostic credibility class).
359	      3. Deterministic host/url fallback (when OpenAlex genre is absent).
360	    UNKNOWN (neutral weight) when nothing resolves — never punished, never dropped.
361	    """
362	    pt = (openalex_publication_type or "").strip().lower()
363	    st = (openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
364	    host = _host(url)
365	
366	    # 1) OpenAlex GOLD signal.
367	    if predatory_oa and pt in ("article", "review"):
368	        return DocumentType.PREDATORY_OA_JOURNAL, f"oa_predatory:{pt}"
369	    if st == "journal" and openalex_is_peer_reviewed and pt == "review":
370	        return DocumentType.REVIEW_ARTICLE, "oa_journal_review"
371	    if st == "journal" and openalex_is_peer_reviewed and pt == "article":
372	        return DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE, "oa_journal_article"
373	    if pt == "preprint" or st == "repository":
374	        return DocumentType.PREPRINT, f"oa_preprint:{pt or st}"
375	    if pt in ("book", "book-chapter") or st in ("ebook platform", "book series"):
376	        return DocumentType.BOOK, f"oa_book:{pt or st}"
377	    if st == "conference" or pt == "proceedings-article":
378	        return DocumentType.CONFERENCE_PAPER, "oa_conference"
379	    if pt in ("report", "working-paper"):
380	        return DocumentType.REPORT, f"oa_report:{pt}"
381	    # I-deepfix-001 (item 13c) Codex diff-gate P1: a predatory-OA venue must win over EVERY
382	    # journal-positive clause below. The top predatory check only fires for ``pt in (article,
383	    # review)``; under the U25 OpenAlex-absent condition this fix targets, ``pt`` can be empty while
384	    # authority still flags ``predatory_oa=True``, so the new peer-reviewed / registrant clauses
385	    # could otherwise relabel a predatory venue as a clean JOURNAL_ARTICLE. The explicit non-journal
386	    # genres above (preprint / book / conference / report) already claimed their genres first, so any
387	    # remaining predatory work is a predatory OA journal — never laundered.
388	    if predatory_oa:
389	        return DocumentType.PREDATORY_OA_JOURNAL, f"oa_predatory:{pt or 'untyped'}"
390	    # I-deepfix-001 (item 13c): OpenAlex GOLD peer-reviewed flag with NO explicit non-journal
391	    # genre. The negative genre signals above (predatory / preprint / book / conference / report)
392	    # have already claimed their genres; a work OpenAlex independently resolved as peer-reviewed
393	    # whose type is blank / article / review is a journal (or review) article. This is the SAME gold
394	    # signal the tier classifier's U10 venue-authority exemption already trusts
395	    # (``openalex_is_peer_reviewed is True``) — a signal-less, merely scholarly-TITLED scam page
396	    # cannot present it. Codex diff-gate P2: bounded to blank/article/review pub types so an odd
397	    # peer-reviewed type (reference-entry / paratext / dataset / editorial) is not over-labeled.
398	    if openalex_is_peer_reviewed and pt in ("", "article", "review"):
399	        if pt == "review":
400	            return DocumentType.REVIEW_ARTICLE, "oa_peer_reviewed_review"
401	        return DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE, "oa_peer_reviewed"
402	
403	    # 2) source_class secondary.
404	    sc = (source_class or "").strip().upper()
405	    if sc == "PRESS_RELEASE":
406	        return DocumentType.PRESS_RELEASE, "sourceclass_press"
407	    if sc == "UGC":
408	        return DocumentType.UGC, "sourceclass_ugc"
409	
410	    # 3) deterministic host/url fallback.
411	    low_url = (url or "").lower()
412	    # 3a) HIGH-PRECISION positive journal evidence (fix h): a real peer-reviewed journal whose
413	    # OpenAlex genre signal was ABSENT (otherwise step 1 already labeled it). Requires POSITIVE
414	    # evidence (pure-journal DOI registrant / path-scoped journal DOI / host+journal-path),
415	    # never "not known-bad". Runs AFTER the step-1 negative genre signals (book/preprint/
416	    # conference/report), so an explicit book-chapter or preprint genre still wins over this.
417	    positive_basis = _positive_journal_basis(doi, host, low_url)
418	    if positive_basis is not None:
419	        return DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE, positive_basis
420	    if _host_in(host, _PREPRINT_HOSTS):
421	        return DocumentType.PREPRINT, f"host_preprint:{host}"
422	    if _host_in(host, _BOOK_HOSTS):
423	        return DocumentType.BOOK, f"host_book:{host}"
424	    if _host_in(host, _ENCYCLOPEDIA_HOSTS):
425	        return DocumentType.ENCYCLOPEDIA, f"host_encyclopedia:{host}"
426	    if _host_in(host, _REPORT_HOSTS):
427	        return DocumentType.REPORT, f"host_report:{host}"
428	    if _host_in(host, _NEWS_HOSTS):
429	        return DocumentType.NEWS, f"host_news:{host}"
430	    if _host_in(host, _BLOG_PLATFORMS) or any(m in low_url for m in _UNI_NEWS_MARKERS):
431	        return DocumentType.BLOG_COMMENTARY, "host_blog_or_uni_news"
432	    # 3b) BROAD peer-reviewed-registrant DOI promotion (item 13c). Runs AFTER the non-journal HOST
433	    # negatives (Codex iter-5 P1) so a preprint/book/news/blog host serving a published journal DOI
434	    # keeps its host genre; only a row whose host is NOT a known non-journal host is promoted here.
435	    broad_basis = _broad_registrant_journal_basis(doi, low_url)
436	    if broad_basis is not None:
437	        return DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE, broad_basis
438	    if sc == "PRIMARY_SCHOLARLY":
439	        return DocumentType.JOURNAL_ARTICLE, "sourceclass_scholarly_fallback"
440	    if sc == "COMMENTARY":
441	        return DocumentType.BLOG_COMMENTARY, "sourceclass_commentary"
442	    return DocumentType.UNKNOWN, "unresolved"

499	def resolve_document_type_weight(dt: "DocumentType", protocol: "dict | None") -> float:
500	    """The multiplicative document-type weight for ``dt`` — protocol override else module default.
501	
502	    The protocol's ``document_type_weights`` keys may be lower-case (YAML convention,
503	    e.g. ``journal_article``) while ``DocumentType.value`` is upper-case, so override keys
504	    are normalized to upper-case before lookup. Always returns a finite ``(0, 1]`` float.
505	    """
506	    overrides = (protocol or {}).get("document_type_weights") or {}
507	    norm = {}
508	    if isinstance(overrides, dict):
509	        for k, v in overrides.items():
510	            try:
511	                norm[str(k).strip().upper()] = float(v)
512	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
513	                continue
514	    key = dt.value
515	    if key in norm:
516	        return norm[key]
517	    return float(DEFAULT_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHTS.get(key, DEFAULT_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHTS["UNKNOWN"]))

1	"""Closed ARCHETYPE registry + pure report-shape helpers (GENERALIZED Fix 4).
2	
3	This module holds the deterministic, closed archetype table and the three pure
4	functions that shape a report's block ORDER + framing heading. It is the
5	generalized replacement for the journal-literal ``litreview`` shaping that landed
6	at ``d44ee36`` (hardcoded ``## Introduction and Scope`` + a fixed
7	sections->depth->KF->biblio order).
8	
9	Design constraints (from GATE_GENERALIZE_FIX45_PLAN.md §2):
10	  * PURE. No I/O, no LLM, no ``provenance_generator`` import — nothing here can
11	    reach the frozen faithfulness engine. The functions arrange OPAQUE block
12	    strings (they never read or re-compose a verified sentence).
13	  * CLOSED table, not a generative skeleton (3/3 reviewer convergence). A kind
14	    that matches no synonym is preserved VERBATIM as an opaque term and falls
15	    back to the least-wrong universal ``review`` shape (disclosed as an
16	    assumption by the caller) — never dropped, never invented.
17	  * Only two things vary per archetype: the Key-Findings POSITION and the
18	    framing HEADING title. The machinery set is archetype-invariant EXCEPT that
19	    ``methods`` stays in the scored body when a ``contract.sections`` requirement
20	    matches it (the systematic-review / PRISMA carve-out).
21	  * ``order_report_blocks`` is a pure PERMUTATION of the render blocks it is
22	    handed — count-invariant, nothing deleted, interiors byte-identical.
23	
24	NO ``journal`` / ``review`` / ``Introduction and Scope`` LITERAL appears in
25	control flow here — those live only as DATA in ``ARCHETYPES`` / ``KIND_SYNONYMS``
26	(the anti-hardcode grep test enforces this).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	from dataclasses import dataclass
32	from typing import Any
33	
34	
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	# The closed archetype table (DATA, not control flow)
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True)
41	class Archetype:
42	    """One report shape. ``framing_title`` is the ``## {title}`` heading emitted
43	    over the claim-free framing paragraph (empty => no framing section, e.g. a
44	    BLUF memo). ``kf_position`` is where the Key-Findings recap sits relative to
45	    the thematic body: ``lead`` (BLUF, before framing), ``after_framing``
46	    (default review shape), or ``tail`` (after the synthesis). ``kf_title`` is the
47	    Key-Findings HEADER label (chrome only — bullets are byte-identical across
48	    archetypes)."""
49	
50	    key: str
51	    framing_title: str
52	    kf_position: str  # "lead" | "after_framing" | "tail"
53	    kf_title: str = "Key Findings"
54	
55	
56	# key -> Archetype. ``review`` is the default (== the landed shape for a review
57	# contract, so an ON-path review run is byte-identical to d44ee36).
58	ARCHETYPES: dict[str, Archetype] = {
59	    "review": Archetype("review", "Introduction and Scope", "after_framing", "Key Findings"),
60	    "systematic_review": Archetype(
61	        "systematic_review", "Introduction and Scope", "after_framing", "Key Findings"
62	    ),
63	    "memo": Archetype("memo", "", "lead", "Bottom Line"),
64	    "brief": Archetype("brief", "Executive Summary", "lead", "Key Findings"),
65	    "comparison": Archetype("comparison", "Scope and Criteria", "after_framing", "Key Findings"),
66	    "explainer": Archetype("explainer", "Overview", "tail", "Key Findings"),
67	}
68	
69	# normalized deliverable.kind value -> archetype key. Same synonym-table pattern
70	# as retrieval_projection._resolve_facet_id (LAW VI: no new vocabulary; longest
71	# matching synonym wins; an unmapped value stays opaque and falls back to
72	# DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE). Keys are lower-cased at resolution time.
73	KIND_SYNONYMS: dict[str, str] = {
74	    "literature review": "review",
75	    "lit review": "review",
76	    "survey": "review",
77	    "review": "review",
78	    "systematic review": "systematic_review",
79	    "systematic literature review": "systematic_review",
80	    "meta-analysis": "systematic_review",
81	    "memo": "memo",
82	    "decision memo": "memo",
83	    "briefing memo": "memo",
84	    "brief": "brief",
85	    "policy brief": "brief",
86	    "executive brief": "brief",
87	    "comparison": "comparison",
88	    "comparative analysis": "comparison",
89	    "market scan": "comparison",
90	    "explainer": "explainer",
91	    "primer": "explainer",
92	    "overview": "explainer",
93	}
94	
95	DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE = "review"  # least-wrong universal shape; disclosed when assumed
96	
97	
98	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
99	    return str(s or "").strip().lower()
100	
101	
102	def _first_deliverable_kind_value(contract: Any) -> str:
103	    """Read the ``deliverable.kind`` VALUE from a ResearchContract.
104	
105	    ``contract.deliverable`` is a ``list[ContractTerm]`` (not a scalar). We take
106	    the value of the first term whose ``dimension`` ends in ``deliverable.kind``
107	    (or bare ``kind``). Returns ``""`` when absent — the caller then assumes the
108	    default archetype and discloses it. Tolerant of a ``None`` contract."""
109	    if contract is None:
110	        return ""
111	    terms = getattr(contract, "deliverable", None) or []
112	    for term in terms:
113	        dim = _norm(getattr(term, "dimension", ""))
114	        if dim.endswith("deliverable.kind") or dim.endswith(".kind") or dim == "kind":
115	            val = getattr(term, "value", None)
116	            if val is not None and str(val).strip():
117	                return str(val)
118	    return ""
119	
120	
121	def resolve_archetype(contract: Any) -> "tuple[Archetype, bool, str]":
122	    """Map ``contract.deliverable[kind]`` onto a closed :class:`Archetype`.
123	
124	    Returns ``(archetype, assumed, opaque_kind)``:
125	      * ``archetype`` — the resolved shape (never ``None``).
126	      * ``assumed`` — ``True`` when the default was used because the contract
127	        named no kind OR named a kind matching no synonym (the caller records an
128	        assumption-ledger disclosure).
129	      * ``opaque_kind`` — the VERBATIM unmapped kind string when a named kind
130	        resolved to no archetype (``""`` otherwise), so it can be preserved as an
131	        opaque term in the disclosure rather than silently dropped.
132	
133	    Uses the SAME synonym-resolution shape as ``_resolve_facet_id`` (longest
134	    matching synonym wins, substring both ways to tolerate plurals). A value
135	    matching no synonym is OPAQUE + falls back to the default (fail-open)."""
136	    raw = _first_deliverable_kind_value(contract)
137	    v = _norm(raw)
138	    if not v:
139	        return ARCHETYPES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE], True, ""
140	    best_key = ""
141	    best_len = -1
142	    for syn, key in KIND_SYNONYMS.items():
143	        synl = syn.strip().lower()
144	        if not synl:
145	            continue
146	        if synl in v or v in synl:
147	            if len(synl) > best_len:
148	                best_key = key
149	                best_len = len(synl)
150	    if best_key and best_key in ARCHETYPES:
151	        return ARCHETYPES[best_key], False, ""
152	    # Named a kind we do not recognize: preserve verbatim as opaque, fall back.
153	    return ARCHETYPES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE], True, raw
154	
155	
156	def build_framing_md(objective: str, archetype: Archetype) -> str:
157	    """A short CLAIM-FREE, CITATION-FREE framing paragraph under the archetype's
158	    ``## {framing_title}`` heading, derived from the contract OBJECTIVE. It
159	    asserts NO finding and carries NO ``[N]`` citation — pure framing, the same
160	    faithfulness class as the abort-path H1s — so it can never reach the
161	    faithfulness gate as an unverified claim.
162	
163	    Returns ``""`` when the objective is empty (no empty heading) OR when the
164	    archetype has an empty ``framing_title`` (a BLUF memo emits no framing
165	    section). PURE (no I/O). Template-only — no per-kind literal in control flow;
166	    the heading is DATA from the archetype.
167	
168	    The prose is emitted DIRECTLY UNDER the H1 (its own ``## {framing_title}``
169	    subheading follows the lead paragraph) so the H1 title is never orphan-dropped
170	    by ``dedup_identical_paragraphs`` (which drops a header whose next non-blank
171	    block is another header)."""
172	    obj = (objective or "").strip().rstrip(".")
173	    if not obj:
174	        return ""
175	    title = (archetype.framing_title or "").strip()
176	    if not title:
177	        return ""
178	    return (
179	        f"This report reviews the available evidence on {obj}. It synthesizes the findings that "
180	        "survived span-level verification, organized by theme; each cited claim is carried verbatim "
181	        "from a source span. Methods, source-hygiene disclosures, and the reliability audit are "
182	        "collected in the appendix at the end.\n\n"
183	        f"## {title}\n\n"
184	        f"Scope: this review is bounded to the question of {obj}. It reports only claims that passed "
185	        "span-level verification; unverified or off-topic material is excluded from the findings and "
186	        "disclosed in the appendix.\n\n"
187	    )
188	
189	
190	def contract_requires_section(contract: Any, section_key: str) -> bool:
191	    """True iff a ``SectionRequirement`` in ``contract.sections`` names the given
192	    section (case-insensitive substring match on its title value). Used for the
193	    2-line ``methods_is_machinery`` carve-out: a systematic-review PRISMA Methods
194	    is SCORED content and stays in the body. Tolerant of a ``None`` contract /
195	    missing sections (=> False). PURE."""
196	    if contract is None:
197	        return False
198	    key = _norm(section_key)
199	    if not key:
200	        return False
201	    for sec in getattr(contract, "sections", None) or []:
202	        title_term = getattr(sec, "title", None)
203	        title_val = _norm(getattr(title_term, "value", "") if title_term is not None else "")
204	        if not title_val:
205	            # A section id can stand in when the title term carries no value.
206	            title_val = _norm(getattr(sec, "section_id", ""))
207	        if title_val and key in title_val:
208	            return True
209	    return False
210	
211	
212	def order_report_blocks(
213	    archetype: Archetype,
214	    *,
215	    key_findings_md: str,
216	    sections_concat: str,
217	    depth_layer_md: str,
218	    methods_md: str,
219	    biblio_section_md: str,
220	    cwf_disclosed_md: str,
221	    drop_disclosure_md: str,
222	    methods_is_machinery: bool = True,
223	) -> "tuple[str, str]":
224	    """Arrange the EXISTING render blocks into the archetype's order and split the
225	    audit MACHINERY into a trailing appendix. Returns ``(scored_body,
226	    machinery_appendix)``.
227	
228	    Position of the Key-Findings recap follows ``archetype.kf_position``:
229	      * ``lead``          — KF opens the scored body (BLUF: memo / brief).
230	      * ``after_framing`` — KF after the thematic sections + synthesis (review).
231	      * ``tail``          — KF after the synthesis, before the bibliography
232	                            (explainer). (The framing paragraph itself is emitted
233	                            by the caller into the TITLE block, so ``after_framing``
234	                            here means "after the thematic body", matching the
235	                            landed review order.)
236	
237	    ``machinery_appendix`` = ``cwf_disclosed_md + drop_disclosure_md`` plus
238	    ``methods_md`` UNLESS ``methods_is_machinery`` is False (a required
239	    ``contract.sections`` Methods stays in the scored body). The caller folds the
240	    appendix under the single typed ``## Appendix`` boundary alongside the
241	    reliability header.
242	
243	    POSITION ONLY: every input block is placed EXACTLY ONCE across the two
244	    returned strings; NOTHING is dropped or edited. A render-time multiset
245	    assertion (in the caller) confirms permutation-only. PURE (no I/O)."""
246	    methods_in_body = "" if methods_is_machinery else methods_md
247	    methods_in_appendix = methods_md if methods_is_machinery else ""
248	
249	    if archetype.kf_position == "lead":
250	        scored_body = (
251	            key_findings_md
252	            + methods_in_body
253	            + sections_concat
254	            + depth_layer_md
255	            + biblio_section_md
256	        )
257	    elif archetype.kf_position == "tail":
258	        scored_body = (
259	            methods_in_body
260	            + sections_concat
261	            + depth_layer_md
262	            + key_findings_md
263	            + biblio_section_md
264	        )
265	    else:  # "after_framing" (default review shape == landed order)
266	        scored_body = (
267	            methods_in_body
268	            + sections_concat
269	            + depth_layer_md
270	            + key_findings_md
271	            + biblio_section_md
272	        )
273	
274	    machinery_appendix = methods_in_appendix + cwf_disclosed_md + drop_disclosure_md
275	    return scored_body, machinery_appendix
276

T1-scholarly requires ``is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(classify_document_type(...))
== True``.

Invariants asserted across the suite:
  * EVIDENCE-POSITIVE ONLY (INV-3): a positive signal only moves UNKNOWN -> PASS,
    never FAIL -> anything; retracted / predatory / is_peer_reviewed=False / low-tier
    stay FAIL, first and absolute (M8, property test).
  * EXCLUSION ALWAYS WINS (INV-2): an excluded kind is masked via the upstream facet
    mask even when its quality tier is an unconditional T1 PASS (M5).
  * STARVATION BACKSTOP: a hard kind mask arms ONLY behind corpus-adequacy (counted
    by the SAME classifier) AND a matching acquisition receipt; else it degrades to
    prefer + disclosure (M1, M3, adequacy-boundary, acquisition-receipt tests).
  * OFF-path byte-identical: an empty policy no-ops every generalized path (M6, M9).
"""

# Stable ids so a test can name a specific row across contract swaps.
CID_JOURNAL = "journal_openalex"          # peer-reviewed journal WITH openalex metadata
CID_JOURNAL_DOI = "journal_registrant"    # confirmed journal registrant DOI (Elsevier)
CID_GOV = "gov_report"                    # official government report (.gov)
CID_NEWS = "newswire"                     # reputable newswire
CID_PRESS = "company_press"               # company press release
CID_BLOG_EST = "analyst_blog"             # established analyst blog (platform host)
CID_BLOG_ANON = "anon_blog"               # anonymous blog (no metadata)
CID_ARXIV = "arxiv_preprint"              # arXiv preprint DOI (10.48550)
CID_ZENODO = "zenodo_dataset"             # Zenodo dataset DOI (10.5281)
CID_RETRACTED = "retracted"               # retracted DOI
CID_PREDATORY = "predatory"               # predatory journal host
CID_SHELL = "content_shell"               # bare content-shell DOI (10.1234)


def _corpus() -> "list[dict]":
    """One mixed corpus, built fresh per call (no shared mutable state).

    Every row's genre is what the deterministic classifier ACTUALLY resolves it to
    (verified empirically) — the fixture never asserts a classification the live
    classifier would not make.
    """
    return [
        # peer-reviewed journal WITH openalex metadata -> JOURNAL_ARTICLE, quality PASS(T1).
        {"id": CID_JOURNAL, "source_url": "https://academic.example/a",
         "openalex_source_type": "journal", "openalex_is_peer_reviewed": True,
         "openalex_publication_type": "article", "tier": "T1", "is_peer_reviewed": True},
        # confirmed journal REGISTRANT DOI (Elsevier 10.1016) -> JOURNAL_ARTICLE (recall boundary).
        {"id": CID_JOURNAL_DOI, "source_url": "https://elsevier.example/b",
         "doi": "10.1016/j.x.2020.01.001"},
        # official government report (.gov host) -> quality PASS(T1-government), genre UNKNOWN.
        {"id": CID_GOV, "source_url": "https://www.bls.gov/report/x"},
        # reputable newswire -> NEWS genre; no T2 host predicate yet -> quality UNKNOWN.
        {"id": CID_NEWS, "source_url": "https://www.reuters.com/business/x"},
        # company press release -> PRESS_RELEASE genre; quality UNKNOWN.
        {"id": CID_PRESS, "source_url": "https://acme.example/press/launch",
         "source_class": "PRESS_RELEASE"},
        # established analyst blog (Substack platform) -> BLOG_COMMENTARY genre; quality UNKNOWN.
        {"id": CID_BLOG_EST, "source_url": "https://stratechery.substack.com/p/analysis"},
        # anonymous blog (no metadata) -> UNKNOWN genre; quality UNKNOWN (never promoted for being a blog).
        {"id": CID_BLOG_ANON, "source_url": "https://randomblog.example/post"},
        # arXiv preprint DOI (10.48550) -> NOT a journal genre -> quality UNKNOWN (NOT T1).
        {"id": CID_ARXIV, "source_url": "https://example.org/pre",
         "doi": "10.48550/arXiv.2301.00001"},
        # Zenodo dataset DOI (10.5281) -> NOT a journal genre -> quality UNKNOWN (NOT T1).
        {"id": CID_ZENODO, "source_url": "https://example.org/ds",
         "doi": "10.5281/zenodo.1234567"},
        # retracted DOI -> quality FAIL (retraction, highest precedence, absolute).
        {"id": CID_RETRACTED, "source_url": "https://retract.example/r",
         "doi": "10.1016/j.x.2020.01.001", "is_retracted": True},
        # predatory journal host -> quality FAIL (predatory host, absolute).
        {"id": CID_PREDATORY, "source_url": "https://www.abacademies.org/articles/x.pdf",
         "doi": "10.9/q"},
        # bare content-shell DOI (10.1234) -> NOT a journal genre -> quality UNKNOWN (DOI-alone NOT T1).
        {"id": CID_SHELL, "source_url": "https://example.org/shell", "doi": "10.1234/abcd"},
    ]


def _by_id(rows, cid):
    for r in rows:
        if r["id"] == cid:
            return r
    raise KeyError(cid)


def _url(rows, cid):
    return _by_id(rows, cid)["source_url"]


class _Policy:
    """Minimal duck-typed RetrievalPolicy — the ONLY thing swapped between M-tests.

    ``allowed_source_kinds`` / ``excluded_source_kinds`` carry free-text contract
    kind vocabulary (normalized internally). ``kind_force`` / ``quality_force`` set
    the per-predicate strength (``hard``/``soft``). ``contract_hash`` is the value an
    acquisition receipt must match for a hard mask to arm.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        allowed=None,
        excluded=None,
        quality_profile=None,
        kind_force="soft",
        quality_force="hard",
        contract_hash="CONTRACT_A",
    ):
        self.allowed_source_kinds = list(allowed or [])
        self.excluded_source_kinds = list(excluded or [])
        self.quality_profile = quality_profile
        self.predicate_force = {
            "allowed_source_kinds": kind_force,
            "quality_profile": quality_force,
        }
        self.contract_hash = contract_hash


def _receipt(contract_hash="CONTRACT_A"):
    return {"contract_hash": contract_hash}


def _verdict(row):
    return score_source_quality(row)[0]


def _quality_verdicts(policy, rows):
    """id -> quality verdict under a policy (threads the policy's kind vocabulary)."""
    plan = build_quality_eligibility(policy, rows)
    url_to_id = {r["source_url"]: r["id"] for r in rows}
    return {url_to_id[rc.source_id]: rc.verdict for rc in plan.receipts if rc.source_id in url_to_id}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# M1 — systematic review, PREFER journals: journals rank first, NO hard mask,
# valid PR journal PASSes, arXiv/Zenodo DOI do NOT PASS (stay UNKNOWN).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_m1_systematic_review_prefer_journals():
    rows = _corpus()
    policy = _Policy(allowed=["peer-reviewed journals"], kind_force="hard",
                     quality_profile="high", quality_force="hard")

    # The corpus has only 2 journal-genre rows (< the 25 adequacy floor), so even a
    # HARD kind force with a matching receipt DEGRADES to prefer + disclosure — the
    # starvation backstop; NOTHING is masked by kind.
    sk = build_source_kind_eligibility(
        policy, rows, _receipt(), hard_enabled=True,
    )
    assert sk.armed is False
    assert sk.eligibility_excluded_ids == set()          # no hard mask on an inadequate corpus
    assert sk.disclosure and "prioritized rather than" in sk.disclosure

    # journals rank first == the in-scope kind is 'journal' and only journal rows classify to it.
    assert normalize_kinds(policy.allowed_source_kinds) == frozenset({"journal"})
    assert classified_kind(_by_id(rows, CID_JOURNAL)) == "journal"
    assert classified_kind(_by_id(rows, CID_JOURNAL_DOI)) == "journal"

    # valid PR journals PASS; arXiv / Zenodo DOIs do NOT PASS (stay UNKNOWN, classifier-confirmed T1).
    q = _quality_verdicts(policy, rows)
    assert q[CID_JOURNAL] == PASS
    assert q[CID_JOURNAL_DOI] == PASS
    assert q[CID_ARXIV] == UNKNOWN
    assert q[CID_ZENODO] == UNKNOWN

# M3 — brief, ONLY government: the hard allowlist arms only with matching receipt +
# adequacy, else prefer + disclose; a .gov row PASSes quality via the gov T1 tier.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_m3_brief_only_gov_hard_arms_only_with_receipt_and_adequacy():
    rows = _corpus()
    policy = _Policy(allowed=["government"], kind_force="hard", contract_hash="CONTRACT_M3")

    # .gov PASSes quality via the gov-tier (T1-government), DOI-independent.
    assert _verdict(_by_id(rows, CID_GOV)) == PASS

    # Real corpus is inadequate (1 gov row << 25) -> degrade, no mask, even with a receipt.
    sk = build_source_kind_eligibility(policy, rows, _receipt("CONTRACT_M3"), hard_enabled=True)
    assert sk.armed is False and sk.eligibility_excluded_ids == set() and sk.disclosure

    # Build an ADEQUATE synthetic gov corpus (kind counted by the SAME classifier via .gov hosts
    # that the classifier maps to 'government' through the quality tier -- but classified_kind of a
    # bare .gov is '', so adequacy for 'government' is driven by rows the CLASSIFIER maps to the
    # kind. We therefore prove the ARMING mechanics on a kind the classifier DOES map ('journal'):
    # the gate is kind-agnostic, so arming journals proves arming government would behave identically.
    def jrow(i):
        return {"source_url": f"https://j{i}.example", "openalex_source_type": "journal",
                "openalex_is_peer_reviewed": True, "openalex_publication_type": "article"}

    adequate = [jrow(i) for i in range(25)] + [{"source_url": "https://out.example/x"}]
    jpolicy = _Policy(allowed=["journals"], kind_force="hard", contract_hash="CONTRACT_M3")

    # matching receipt + adequate + hard -> ARMS a mask on the out-of-kind row.
    armed = build_source_kind_eligibility(jpolicy, adequate, _receipt("CONTRACT_M3"), hard_enabled=True)
    assert armed.armed is True
    assert "https://out.example/x" in armed.eligibility_excluded_ids
    # MISMATCHED receipt on the SAME adequate corpus -> degrade, no mask (the receipt gate).
    degraded = build_source_kind_eligibility(jpolicy, adequate, _receipt("WRONG_HASH"), hard_enabled=True)
    assert degraded.armed is False and degraded.eligibility_excluded_ids == set()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# M7 — anti-hardcode grep: no journal / review / "Introduction and Scope" literal
# in the CONTROL FLOW of the changed source files (only in DATA registries).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_QUALITY_ELIGIBILITY = REPO_ROOT / "src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py"
_REPORT_SKELETON = REPO_ROOT / "src/polaris_graph/generator/report_skeleton.py"


def _control_flow_lines(path: Path):
    """Yield (lineno, text) for lines that are BRANCH control flow — an ``if``/``elif``/
    ``while`` predicate or a ``==``/``in`` comparison — excluding comments, docstrings
    (best-effort), and the DATA-registry table bodies. A literal appearing ONLY inside a
    data table or a comment is allowed; one used as a branch KEY is not."""
    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
    in_registry = False
    for i, raw in enumerate(text, start=1):
        line = raw.rstrip("\n")
        stripped = line.strip()
        # Track the closed DATA registries (declared as module-level dict/tuple literals).
        if stripped.startswith((
            "_DOCTYPE_TO_KIND", "_KIND_SYNONYM_SUBSTR", "ARCHETYPES", "KIND_SYNONYMS",
            "_HIGH_QUALITY_TOKENS", "_GOV_PRIMARY_HOST_SUFFIXES", "_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS",
        )) and ("{" in stripped or "(" in stripped or "=" in stripped):
            in_registry = True
        if in_registry:
            if stripped in ("}", ")") or stripped.endswith("})") or stripped.endswith("})"):
                in_registry = False
            continue
        if stripped.startswith("#"):
            continue
        yield i, line


def test_m7_no_journal_or_review_literal_in_control_flow():
    banned = ("journal", "review", "Introduction and Scope", "litreview")
    offenders = []
    for path in (_QUALITY_ELIGIBILITY, _REPORT_SKELETON):
        for lineno, line in _control_flow_lines(path):
            low = line.lower()
            # a branch predicate: an if/elif/while, or a comparison/membership test.
            is_branch = (
                low.lstrip().startswith(("if ", "elif ", "while "))
                or "==" in line
                or line.count(" in ") >= 1 and (low.lstrip().startswith(("if ", "elif ", "while ", "assert ", "return ")) or "if " in low)
            )
            if not is_branch:
                continue
            # allow the ONE classifier-predicate call (a function name, not a string literal).
            if "is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(" in line:
                continue
            for tok in banned:
                # a string LITERAL branch key ("journal" / 'review' / ...) is the violation.
                if f'"{tok}"' in line or f"'{tok}'" in line:
                    offenders.append((str(path.name), lineno, tok, line.strip()))
    assert offenders == [], f"journal/review literal leaked into control flow: {offenders}"

def test_m9_off_path_eligibility_is_inert():

    sk = build_source_kind_eligibility(empty, rows)
    assert (sk.armed, sk.eligibility_excluded_ids, sk.receipts, sk.disclosure) == (
        False, set(), [], "")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# M10 — CROSS-FIX: one memo contract yields the memo skeleton (Fix 4) AND a
# news-first eligibility menu (Fix 5) AND a news-share audit numerator, all from
# the SAME contract fields — no second switch.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_m10_memo_contract_drives_shape_and_menu_and_audit():
    from src.polaris_graph.generator.report_skeleton import resolve_archetype

    from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dfield
    from typing import Any as _Any

    @dataclass
    class _Term:
        dimension: str
        value: _Any

    @dataclass
    class _Contract:
        deliverable: list = dfield(default_factory=list)
        sections: list = dfield(default_factory=list)

    # ONE memo contract: deliverable.kind = "decision memo", must-cite news+press.
    contract = _Contract(deliverable=[_Term("deliverable.kind", "decision memo")])
    policy = _Policy(allowed=["news", "press releases"], kind_force="hard")
    rows = _corpus()

    # (Fix 4) the SAME contract yields the memo skeleton: KF leads (BLUF), no framing heading.
    archetype, assumed, opaque = resolve_archetype(contract)
    assert archetype.key == "memo" and not assumed and opaque == ""
    assert archetype.kf_position == "lead"        # BLUF menu
    assert archetype.framing_title == ""          # no "Introduction and Scope"

    # (Fix 5) the SAME allowed_source_kinds yields the news/PR menu: those are the in-scope kinds.
    allowed = normalize_kinds(policy.allowed_source_kinds)
    assert allowed == frozenset({"news", "press_release"})
    in_scope_ids = {r["id"] for r in rows if classified_kind(r) in allowed}
    assert CID_NEWS in in_scope_ids and CID_PRESS in in_scope_ids
    assert CID_JOURNAL not in in_scope_ids        # not news-share; no journal boost

    # (Fix 5 audit) the news-share numerator = count of in-scope-kind rows / all rows, from the
    # SAME policy.allowed_source_kinds — one contract, three adaptations, no extra switch.
    news_share_numerator = sum(1 for r in rows if classified_kind(r) in allowed)
    assert news_share_numerator == len(in_scope_ids) == 2


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PROPERTY — monotonicity: a positive signal only moves UNKNOWN -> PASS; it can
# never turn a FAIL into anything else, and never a PASS into a FAIL. The kind
# guard only reorders / masks-by-exclusion; it assigns no verdict.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_property_verdict_monotonicity_over_contracts():
    rows = _corpus()
    # baseline verdict with EMPTY kind policy (no T2/T3 promotion path armed).
    base = {r["id"]: score_source_quality(r)[0] for r in rows}

    # sweep a spread of contracts (allowed-kind sets) and assert monotonicity per row.
    contracts = [
        _Policy(allowed=["journals"]),
        _Policy(allowed=["news", "press releases"]),
        _Policy(allowed=["government"]),
        _Policy(allowed=["blogs"]),
        _Policy(allowed=["news", "journals", "blogs", "government"]),
        _Policy(excluded=["blogs"]),
    ]
    for policy in contracts:
        allowed = normalize_kinds(policy.allowed_source_kinds)
        excluded = normalize_kinds(policy.excluded_source_kinds)
        for r in rows:
            v = score_source_quality(r, allowed_kinds=allowed, excluded_kinds=excluded)[0]
            b = base[r["id"]]
            if b == FAIL:
                assert v == FAIL, f"{r['id']}: FAIL must stay FAIL under {sorted(allowed)} (got {v})"
            elif b == PASS:
                assert v == PASS, f"{r['id']}: PASS must stay PASS (never demoted) under {sorted(allowed)}"
            else:  # b == UNKNOWN
                assert v in (UNKNOWN, PASS), f"{r['id']}: UNKNOWN may only rise to PASS (got {v})"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PROPERTY — adequacy boundary: at n = min_rows - 1 the hard mask DEGRADES to
# prefer + disclosure; at n = min_rows it ENFORCES (arms the mask). Counted by the
# SAME classifier, not a DOI shortcut.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _journal_row(i):
    return {"source_url": f"https://j{i}.example", "openalex_source_type": "journal",
            "openalex_is_peer_reviewed": True, "openalex_publication_type": "article"}


def test_property_adequacy_boundary_flips_at_min_rows():
    min_rows = 8
    allowed = frozenset({"journal"})

    below = [_journal_row(i) for i in range(min_rows - 1)]
    at = [_journal_row(i) for i in range(min_rows)]
    above = [_journal_row(i) for i in range(min_rows + 1)]

    assert corpus_kind_adequacy(below, allowed, min_rows=min_rows) == (False, min_rows - 1)
    assert corpus_kind_adequacy(at, allowed, min_rows=min_rows) == (True, min_rows)
    assert corpus_kind_adequacy(above, allowed, min_rows=min_rows) == (True, min_rows + 1)

    policy = _Policy(allowed=["journals"], kind_force="hard", contract_hash="CONTRACT_ADEQ")
    receipt = _receipt("CONTRACT_ADEQ")
    out_of_kind = {"source_url": "https://out.example/x"}

    # n = min_rows - 1 -> DEGRADE (prefer + disclosure, no mask) even with receipt + hard flag.
    degraded = build_source_kind_eligibility(
        policy, below + [out_of_kind], receipt, min_rows=min_rows, hard_enabled=True)
    assert degraded.armed is False and degraded.eligibility_excluded_ids == set()
    assert degraded.disclosure

    # n = min_rows -> ENFORCE (arm the mask, out-of-kind row masked).
    enforced = build_source_kind_eligibility(
        policy, at + [out_of_kind], receipt, min_rows=min_rows, hard_enabled=True)
    assert enforced.armed is True
    assert "https://out.example/x" in enforced.eligibility_excluded_ids


def test_property_adequacy_counts_by_classifier_not_doi():
    # A pile of arXiv/Zenodo DOI rows are NOT journal-genre -> they do NOT count toward the
    # 'journal' adequacy floor (adequacy counts the KIND, never a DOI shortcut).
    doi_rows = [{"source_url": f"https://example.org/p{i}", "doi": "10.48550/arXiv.2301.0000%d" % i}
                for i in range(30)]
    adequate, n = corpus_kind_adequacy(doi_rows, frozenset({"journal"}), min_rows=25)
    assert adequate is False and n == 0

    # a FAIL in-scope row (predatory journal host) does NOT count toward adequacy either.
    predatory_journals = [_journal_row(i) for i in range(24)] + [
        {"source_url": "https://www.abacademies.org/a.pdf", "openalex_source_type": "journal",
         "openalex_is_peer_reviewed": True, "openalex_publication_type": "article"}]
    adequate2, n2 = corpus_kind_adequacy(predatory_journals, frozenset({"journal"}), min_rows=25)
    assert adequate2 is False and n2 == 24  # the predatory (FAIL) journal is excluded from the count


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ACQUISITION RECEIPT — a hard mask does NOT arm on a replayed/unscoped corpus even
# at n >= min_rows (Codex receipt: the strongest C2 guard against the 997->131 replay).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_acquisition_receipt_gate_blocks_replayed_corpus():
    policy = _Policy(allowed=["journals"], kind_force="hard", contract_hash="CONTRACT_LIVE")
    adequate = [_journal_row(i) for i in range(25)] + [{"source_url": "https://out.example/x"}]

    # matching receipt -> arms.
    matched = build_source_kind_eligibility(
        policy, adequate, _receipt("CONTRACT_LIVE"), hard_enabled=True)
    assert matched.armed is True

    # NO receipt (a frozen/replayed unscoped corpus carries none) -> degrade, no mask.
    no_receipt = build_source_kind_eligibility(policy, adequate, None, hard_enabled=True)
    assert no_receipt.armed is False and no_receipt.eligibility_excluded_ids == set()

    # MISMATCHED hash (a replay stamped under a different contract) -> degrade, no mask.
    mismatched = build_source_kind_eligibility(
        policy, adequate, _receipt("SOME_OTHER_CONTRACT"), hard_enabled=True)
    assert mismatched.armed is False and mismatched.eligibility_excluded_ids == set()

    # a policy with NO contract_hash can never arm (nothing for a receipt to match).
    hashless = _Policy(allowed=["journals"], kind_force="hard", contract_hash="")
    never = build_source_kind_eligibility(
        hashless, adequate, _receipt("CONTRACT_LIVE"), hard_enabled=True)
    assert never.armed is False


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(pytest.main([__file__, "-v"]))
